title: Delicate Dutch Bellflower Root Stew
description: A silky, herb-infused Dutch stew featuring tender bellflower root (doraji) and root vegetables, finished with a wine deglaze for nostalgic comfort.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and minced garlic, sautéing until fragrant and translucent.
Stir in the minced ginger, carrots, and potatoes, cooking for 5 minutes.
Pour in the Dutch white wine to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the vegetable stock, bellflower root slices, thyme and bay leaf, salt, and pepper, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot and let the stew cook for 30 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
After 30 minutes, add the butter, stirring until fully melted and incorporated into the broth.
Remove the thyme and bay leaf, then taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Ladle the stew into bowls and garnish with freshly chopped parsley.
Serve hot, accompanied by crusty bread or a side of Dutch rye for a nostalgic finish.
